title: The Invisible Labor Behind Building a Pilates Empire: Paige Johnson's Story
guestBio: Paige Johnson is the co-owner of Mantra Pilates and a former competitive gymnast who transitioned into beloved gymnastics coaching. As a devoted mother and compassionate leader, she's known for her powerful presence and ability to build community through movement while navigating the invisible work of running a business and raising a family.
excerpt: Former gymnast turned Pilates studio co-owner Paige Johnson reveals the unseen labor behind building a fitness empire while raising a daughter. She shares how movement becomes emotional leadership and why women are consistently underestimated in business and fitness spaces.
metaDescription: Pilates studio owner Paige Johnson discusses invisible labor, female leadership in fitness, and building community through movement on The Manage Her podcast.
keyTakeaways: The invisible labor of running a business extends far beyond what clients and customers see on the surface.
Women in fitness and business spaces are often underestimated, but this can become a source of strength and motivation.
Movement isn't just about physical fitness—it's a form of emotional and energetic leadership that impacts every area of life.
Building community through shared experiences creates deeper connections than traditional business networking.
Technology tools can help busy entrepreneurs stay organized and connected while managing multiple responsibilities.
Finding your personal mantra helps you stay grounded when juggling business ownership and motherhood.
Sometimes leadership means knowing when to rise up and when to let go with grace.

pullQuotes.text: Movement isn't just fitness — it's emotional and energetic leadership
It's about the strength it takes to hold it all, and the grace it takes to let some of it go

timestamps.label: The Invisible Labor of Business Ownership
Being Underestimated as a Woman in Fitness
Closing Thoughts and Where to Connect

content:
What does it really take to build a thriving Pilates studio while raising a family? If you've ever wondered about the invisible work behind successful women-owned businesses, this conversation with Paige Johnson will give you all the behind-the-scenes insight you've been craving.

Paige Johnson, co-owner of Mantra Pilates, brings a unique perspective to entrepreneurship that's rooted in movement, community, and authentic leadership. Her journey from competitive gymnast to beloved gymnastics coach to Pilates studio owner illustrates how our paths often weave together in unexpected ways, creating something more powerful than we initially imagined.

The Invisible Architecture of Success

When you walk into a beautifully run Pilates studio, you see the polished result—the seamless classes, the welcoming atmosphere, the sense of community that makes you want to keep coming back. What you don't see is the invisible labor that creates that experience. Paige opens up about this hidden work that keeps both studios and families thriving, from the early morning planning sessions to the late-night problem-solving that happens long after the last client leaves.

This invisible labor extends far beyond scheduling classes and managing instructors. It's about creating an environment where people feel seen, supported, and empowered to show up as their best selves. It's about anticipating needs before they're expressed and building systems that allow both team members and clients to flourish. This kind of leadership requires emotional intelligence, strategic thinking, and an incredible amount of behind-the-scenes work that often goes unrecognized.

Navigating Underestimation as Fuel for Growth

One of the most powerful themes in this conversation is how women are consistently underestimated in both business and fitness spaces. Rather than letting this diminish her confidence, Paige has learned to use these moments as fuel for proving what's possible when women lead with authenticity and strength.

The fitness industry, in particular, can be challenging for women who want to be taken seriously as business owners rather than just pretty faces in activewear. Paige shares how she's navigated these perceptions while building credibility and respect in her field. Her approach isn't about proving herself to doubters—it's about staying focused on the impact she wants to make and the community she's building.

This underestimation often comes from a fundamental misunderstanding of what leadership looks like when it's rooted in compassion and community-building rather than dominance and competition. Paige demonstrates that powerful leadership can be nurturing, inclusive, and strength-based all at the same time.

Movement as More Than Fitness

Perhaps the most transformative insight from this conversation is Paige's perspective on movement as emotional and energetic leadership. This isn't just about getting stronger or more flexible—it's about how the way you move through your workout translates to how you move through your life.

When you commit to showing up for yourself physically, you're practicing the same skills you need to show up as a leader in your business and family. The discipline, consistency, and self-compassion required for a sustainable movement practice are exactly the qualities that make someone an effective leader in other areas of life.

Paige's approach to Pilates isn't just about physical transformation—it's about helping people discover their own strength, both literally and metaphorically. She creates space for people to push their boundaries while honoring their limitations, a skill that translates beautifully to business leadership and parenting.

Technology and Systems for the Busy Entrepreneur

Running a successful business while being present for your family requires more than good intentions—it requires smart systems and strategic use of technology. Paige shares specific tools and approaches that help her stay organized, inspired, and connected without becoming overwhelmed by digital noise.

The key is finding technology solutions that actually simplify your life rather than adding more complexity. This means being intentional about which apps, platforms, and systems you adopt, and regularly evaluating whether they're serving your goals or just creating busy work.

Finding Your Mantra Through Motherhood

The intersection of business ownership and motherhood creates unique challenges that require deep wells of strength and flexibility. Paige's journey to finding her personal mantra—her guiding principle that keeps her grounded—offers valuable insights for any woman trying to balance multiple demanding roles.

A mantra isn't just a pretty phrase you repeat during meditation. It's a touchstone that helps you make decisions, prioritize your energy, and stay connected to your purpose when everything feels chaotic. Paige's process of discovering her mantra through the challenges and joys of motherhood provides a roadmap for other women seeking their own guiding principles.

This conversation beautifully illustrates the truth that leadership isn't about having it all figured out—it's about showing up authentically, building community, and creating space for others to discover their own strength. Paige's story reminds us that the most powerful leaders are often those who understand both when to rise up with fierce determination and when to let go with grace.

Whether you're building a business, raising children, or simply trying to lead a more intentional life, Paige's insights about invisible labor, underestimation, and movement as leadership offer practical wisdom you can apply immediately. Her journey proves that success isn't just about reaching your goals—it's about who you become and the community you build along the way.
